prompt:
CI platform request. Our artifact mirror flakes a few times a day and every team has invented its own retry loop around the uploader, each one differently wrong (one retries on EVERY failure including bad credentials — that one locked an account). I want the one blessed wrapper, checked in: `retry.sh`, repo root, plain bash, no GNU getopt — parse flags with an ordinary loop. It runs on the macOS runners too, portable everything.

Usage: `retry.sh [--max N] [--retry-on codes] [--fatal-on codes] -- cmd [args...]`

The `--` is mandatory; everything after it is the command and is passed through EXACTLY as given — empty args, spaces, things that look like our own flags, all preserved verbatim. The command's stdout must pass through untouched; retry.sh speaks only on stderr.

Exit-code classes: codes are comma-separated positive integers. Default `--retry-on 1,75`, default `--fatal-on 64,78`. After each attempt: exit 0 is success, done. A code in the fatal list stops immediately. A code in the retry list earns another attempt if any remain (--max, default 4, total attempts). A nonzero code in NEITHER list is treated as fatal — unknown failures should page a human, not loop. A code appearing in both lists is a usage error.

Backoff between retryable attempts, fixed table, no jitter (we want log diffs quiet):

    after attempt 1 -> wait 1s
    after attempt 2 -> wait 3s
    after attempt 3 -> wait 7s
    after attempt 4 -> wait 15s
    after attempt 5 and beyond -> wait 30s

Sleeping is INJECTABLE: honor the env var RETRY_SLEEP_CMD, defaulting to `sleep`. Its value is split on whitespace and invoked with the delay in seconds appended as one argument (so `RETRY_SLEEP_CMD='bash fakesleep.sh'` runs `bash fakesleep.sh 3`). The CI test rig stubs it — the acceptance tests must run with ZERO real sleeping. If the sleep command itself fails, stop with `retry: sleep command failed` on stderr, exit 70.

Transcript, one stderr line per attempt, byte-exact:

    retry: attempt 2/4: exit 75 (retryable), sleeping 3s
    retry: attempt 3/4: ok
    retry: attempt 1/4: exit 78 (fatal), giving up
    retry: attempt 4/4: exit 75 (retryable), giving up

That's the four shapes: a retryable failure with attempts left says what it will sleep; success says ok; a fatal (listed-fatal or unlisted) failure says giving up; a retryable failure on the last attempt says giving up too. retry.sh's own exit code is the LAST attempt's exit code (0 on success) — pipelines upstream key off it.

Usage errors (missing --, nothing after --, bad --max, empty/garbage/zero-containing code lists, a code in both lists, unknown flag) print `usage: retry.sh [--max N] [--retry-on codes] [--fatal-on codes] -- cmd [args...]` to stderr and exit 64.

`bash test_retry.sh` from the workspace root is the acceptance gate; it must pass unmodified — don't touch the test file.
